description: You are a Space Situational Awareness (SSA) analyst investigating the Kosmos 1408 ASAT fragmentation event. Using the initial state and requirements in ~/Desktop/kosmos_1408_asat_event.txt, simulate the debris cloud dispersion over 90 days using GMAT. The simulation must include the parent satellite and 20 debris fragments starting with the parent's initial Keplerian elements. Apply velocity-direction kicks in the Local VNB frame to the fragments ranging from -100 m/s to +100 m/s in 10 m/s increments (excluding 0 m/s) at the simulation epoch. Use a force model consisting of Earth point mass and J2 gravity (Degree 2, Order 0) only, disabling drag, SRP, and third-body gravity. Generate a GMAT script at ~/GMAT_output/simulate_debris.script and a report of the final EarthMJ2000Eq.RAAN for all spacecraft at ~/GMAT_output/debris_raan_report.txt. Calculate the maximum RAAN, minimum RAAN, and the total angular spread in degrees, then save these results to ~/GMAT_output/dispersion_summary.json using the keys max_raan_deg, min_raan_deg, and raan_spread_deg. Login: username=ga, password=password123
